description_long: This tunnel structure is long and rectangular, featuring a series of evenly spaced, raised ridges along its top surface, which may serve as reinforcement or for structural stability. The sides are straight and parallel, with a consistent height throughout the length of the tunnel. The ends are flat and open, suggesting a pass-through design suitable for vehicles, pedestrians, or utilities. The exterior appears to be constructed from a dark, likely metallic or reinforced material, giving it a robust and industrial appearance. There are no visible windows, doors, or decorative elements, emphasizing a utilitarian function. The overall design is simple, functional, and optimized for durability in an urban environment.
description: A long, rectangular tunnel with raised ridges along the top and open, flat ends, constructed from a dark, robust material. Its design is utilitarian and industrial, with no decorative features.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the tunnel and the evenly spaced ridges along the top.
description_view_1: End view displaying the flat, open entrance and the alignment of the ridges from front to back.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with the same length and ridge pattern visible.
description_view_3: Rear end view, similar to view 1, showing the open exit and the tunnel's internal passage.
attribute_entrance_type: Rectangular open ends, constructed from reinforced steel and concrete, with no doors or gates and a simple, industrial finish.
attribute_surrounding_infrastructure: No visible surrounding infrastructure; the tunnel appears isolated with no adjacent roads, tracks, or barriers.
